title: Go 1.26 Repository, pgx, Migrations, and Reproduction · Contract Triage
summary: A preserved Go feed-service run contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, normalization, fetch, SQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, handler, accessibility, migration, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in reproducible Go feed-service repository baseline and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Capture revision, worktree, configuration, fixtures, secrets, migrations, licenses, and recovery anchor before editing.
Pin Go 1.26.5, pgx v5.10.0, goose v3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, module sums, and supported platforms.
Keep command, application, domain, repository, fetch, parser, scheduler, HTTP, and presentation dependencies directed inward.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 1968 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile Go, pgx, migration, dependency, clean-build, and artifact evidence; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Clean Gates
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: feed-go-clean-gates-1ckjkp" that uses case 7691 to reconcile Go, pgx, migration, dependency, clean-build, and artifact evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, launch unbounded goroutines, or claim race, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, pgx 5.10.0, goose 3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, disposable databases, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected images, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.
